PR surface: Source +1. Total +1 across 2 files.

target.title: fix(browser): correct ssrf-runtime import path to fix lazy-load failure
target.body:
Closes #96639

## What Problem This Solves

The `ssrf-runtime-internal` module import path used `./plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal` which is not defined in the package `"exports"` field, causing a lazy-load failure at runtime when the browser plugin attempts to dynamically import the SSRF runtime.

## Why This Change Was Made

Corrected the import path from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal` to `openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime` to match the export map in package.json so the lazy-load succeeds.

## Evidence

- The corrected path matches the actual export definition in the Plugin SDK package.json
- TypeScript compilation passes for the changed file
- The function signature of `registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass` is identical between the two export paths

### Not tested
- Full browser plugin lazy-load integration (requires live browser infrastructure)

## Merge Risk

**Low**. The change:
- One line: only the import path string
- The exported function signature is unchanged
- Uses the public export path instead of the internal one

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
